releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/masaki39/simple-citations/compare/1.6.1...1.6.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Simple Citations 1.6.1

## Bug Fixes

### BetterBibTeX JSON: year parsing for MM/YYYY date format
- Fixed an issue where dates in `MM/YYYY` format (e.g. `"07/2019"`) were parsed as the month number instead of the year.
- The `year` property is now correctly extracted from all common date formats found in BetterBibTeX JSON exports: `YYYY`, `YYYY-MM`, `YYYY-MM-DD`, `YYYY/MM/DD`, and `MM/YYYY`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Simple Citations 1.6.0

## New Features

### BetterBibTeX JSON support
- BetterBibTeX JSON files exported by the Better BibTeX for Zotero extension can now be registered alongside Better CSL JSON files.
- Format is detected automatically from file content — no additional configuration required.
- Field mapping from BetterBibTeX JSON:
  - `creators` (author type) → `authors`
  - `date` → `year`
  - `publicationTitle` → `journal`
  - `DOI` → `doi`
  - `select` → `zotero` (supports both personal and group libraries natively)
- Two new opt-in properties, shown in settings only when a BetterBibTeX JSON file is detected:
  - **Include PDF paths** — automatically populates a `pdf` property from local attachment paths (no postscript required).
  - **Include collections** — automatically populates a `collections` property from Zotero collection membership.
- Disabling either toggle removes the corresponding property from existing notes on the next update.
- Both formats can be used simultaneously. File order in settings determines priority when the same citation key appears in multiple files.

### Pandoc: CSL JSON–only bibliography
- The Pandoc Citeproc command now passes only Better CSL JSON files to `--bibliography`, skipping BetterBibTeX JSON files (which are not supported by citeproc).
- If no CSL JSON file is registered, the command is cancelled with an explanatory notice instead of silently failing.

### PDF export commands (requires Poppler)
- **Export PDF** — copies the PDF file(s) listed in the `pdf` frontmatter property to the configured export folder.
- **Export PDF images** — extracts embedded images from PDF(s) as PNG files using `pdfimages` (part of [Poppler](https://poppler.freedesktop.org)). Output files are named `pdf1-000.png`, `pdf1-001.png`, etc.
- Both commands read the `pdf` property set automatically when using BetterBibTeX JSON, or manually via the optional fields / postscript workflow.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Simple Citations 1.5.1

## Bug Fixes

### Zotero select URL for group libraries
- Fixed incorrect Zotero select URL generation for items in group libraries.
- Previously, group library items used the personal library URL format (`zotero://select/items/@key`), which did not open the correct item in Zotero.
- Now correctly generates `zotero://select/groups/{groupId}/items/@key` when the item belongs to a group library.

## Contributors

- [@haiqiang-zhang](https://github.com/haiqiang-zhang) — [#8](https://github.com/masaki39/simple-citations/pull/8)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Simple Citations 1.5.0

## New Features

### Multi bibliography support
- Multiple Better CSL JSON files can now be registered in settings.
- Files are prioritized by order; earlier entries take precedence when the same citation key appears in multiple files.
- Per-property merge strategies: choose **Priority** (use highest-priority file) or **Merge** (combine values from all files) for each field. `collections` and `library` default to Merge.
- A new **Bibliography** property (opt-in) records which file(s) each citation was sourced from.

### Mobile support
- Settings UI is now responsive and usable on mobile devices.

### New commands
- **Sync citations** — adds new notes and updates existing ones in a single step.
- **Reset all properties (Full sync)** — clears and rewrites all frontmatter properties; shows a confirmation modal before running.

## Contributors

- [@haiqiang-zhang](https://github.com/haiqiang-zhang) — [#7](https://github.com/masaki39/simple-citations/pull/7)

Check missing note links
This feature helps you identify missing note links that are not included in a JSON file.

### New Feature!
Execution Time Display
Now, when you execute commands, the system will display the time taken for execution.

New Features: Abstract and Template Options

We have introduced a new option. Now, you can add Abstract and Template to your literature notes.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
We have introduced a new option. Now, you can add tags for the First Author and Journal.

## New Features:
- **First Author Tag**: This allows you to specify the first author of the referenced document, making it easier to track contributions.
- **Journal Tag**: This tag helps categorize references based on their published journal, facilitating better organization and retrieval.

These additional tags will enhance metadata management and improve the efficiency of literature tracking.
